title: Matrix Dimensions and Properties
description: Explore nrow, ncol, dim, rownames, colnames, and attributes of matrices.

slides.title: Getting Dimensions 📏
slides.content:
Once you have a matrix, you can query its dimensions:

- `nrow(m)` – number of rows
- `ncol(m)` – number of columns
- `dim(m)` – both dimensions as a vector (rows, cols)

```r
m <- matrix(1:12, nrow=3, ncol=4)
nrow(m)   # 3
ncol(m)   # 4
dim(m)    # 3 4
```

slides.title: Check Dimensions
slides.codeSnippet:
m <- matrix(1:12, nrow=3, ncol=4)
print(nrow(m))
print(ncol(m))
print(dim(m))

quizData.question: What is the result of `dim(matrix(1:8, nrow=2))`?
quizData.explanation: If ncol is omitted, it is calculated as length(data)/nrow, so 8/2 = 4. dim returns rows, cols: 2 4.

slides.title: Row and Column Names 🏷️
slides.content:
You can assign names to rows and columns for easier access.

- `rownames(m)` – get or set row names
- `colnames(m)` – get or set column names
- `dimnames(m)` – get or set both as a list

```r
m <- matrix(1:6, nrow=2)
rownames(m) <- c("R1", "R2")
colnames(m) <- c("C1", "C2", "C3")
```

slides.title: Naming Examples
slides.codeSnippet:
sales <- matrix(c(100,150,200,120,180,210), nrow=2, byrow=TRUE)
rownames(sales) <- c("Store_A", "Store_B")
colnames(sales) <- c("Mon", "Tue", "Wed")
print(sales)

# Access names
print(rownames(sales))
print(colnames(sales))

slides.title: Other Attributes
slides.content:
Matrices are just vectors with a `dim` attribute. You can see all attributes with `attributes()`.

```r
attributes(sales)
```

You can also change the mode (e.g., convert to character) but that usually coerces the whole matrix.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
Use `str()` to see a compact summary of a matrix:

```r
str(sales)
```
